assembly_instructions: Read Qwen3.6 as the LIKELY next step beyond Qwen3-235B-A22B and the Qwen3-Next experiments: a HYBRID-ATTENTION MoE that adopts Qwen3-Next's Gated DeltaNet (linear attention) for most layers, with a few full-softmax layers preserved for associative recall. Tokens enter at the floor (X=0,Y=0) and climb the cyan residual pillar through 192 transformer blocks. Pattern: 3 LINEAR (Gated DeltaNet) blocks for every 1 SOFTMAX (Gated Attention) block. Linear blocks are pale-amber and carry a fixed-size recurrent state (the cyan torus at X=-2.5); softmax blocks are deep-crimson and use the conventional GQA KV cache (amber column at X=-3.5). To the right at X=6 sits the per-layer MoE FFN (256 routed experts, top-8, NO shared expert). Above the stack at X=4.5/Y=9.5 is Dual-Chunk Attention extended to 256K native + 1M YaRN. Click any part for details. THIS IS A FORWARD-LOOKING PROJECTION — numbers should be read as plausible extrapolation, not fact.

info.summary: PROJECTED next-generation flagship from Alibaba's Qwen team, extrapolated from the Qwen3 + Qwen3-Next research direction. No public paper at scene time. Best guess: scales Qwen3-Next's hybrid Gated DeltaNet (linear) + Gated Attention (softmax) pattern to flagship size. ~480B total / ~30B activated, 192 layers, 3:1 LINEAR:SOFTMAX attention ratio, 256 routed experts (top-8, no shared), QK-Norm + RoPE on softmax layers, MTP head, /think + /no_think modes preserved, 256K native context (1M with DCA + YaRN), 200+ languages. Treat numbers as plausible extrapolation, not fact.
info.description:
Qwen3.6 is presented here as a research-trajectory PROJECTION, not a paper. Where Qwen3-235B-A22B was full softmax + GQA(64/4) + 128 experts top-8, and where Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B introduced the Gated DeltaNet linear-attention + Gated Attention hybrid at small scale, Qwen3.6 likely (i) brings that hybrid to flagship scale (480B-A30B), (ii) doubles depth (94 → 192 layers — Qwen3-Next is already 48 layers narrow), (iii) doubles expert count (128 → 256), (iv) keeps GQA at extreme 16:1 in the few softmax layers, (v) adds an MTP head (Qwen3 lacked one; Qwen3-Coder added one — full family adoption), (vi) extends native context 32K → 256K with DCA + YaRN reaching 1M, (vii) keeps /think + /no_think system-tag modes.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• Gated DeltaNet (linear): O(N) attention for 75% of layers — KV-cache memory scales with layer count, not context length, on those layers.
• Keep some softmax: pure linear attention loses associative recall (the 'needle in a haystack' problem); 1-in-4 softmax layer restores it.
• Doubled experts (256): adds capacity at the same active-FLOPs budget.
• MTP added: ~1.8× speculative decoding for free.
• 192 layers: Qwen3-Next showed deep-narrow scaling holds; flagship inherits.

facts.label: context length
facts.value: 256 K native, 1 M with DCA + YaRN

facts.label: softmax heads
facts.value: 64 Q / 4 KV (GQA, ratio 16 — kept from Qwen3)

facts.label: precision
facts.value: FP8 GEMMs + BF16 master, INT4 KV (projected)

comparisons.delta: Qwen3-235B was full-softmax GQA(64/4) + 128 experts top-8 + no MTP, 94 layers. Projected Qwen3.6 doubles total params (235B → 480B), modestly grows activated (22B → 30B), DOUBLES depth (94 → 192), DOUBLES experts (128 → 256), introduces the 3:1 linear/softmax hybrid borrowed from Qwen3-Next, adds an MTP head, and pushes context from 32K-trained / 128K-extended to 256K-trained / 1M-extended. /think+/no_think modes preserved.

Qwen3-Next was the small (80B-A3B) Qwen3 prototype that introduced Gated DeltaNet + Gated Attention at a 3:1 ratio over 48 layers. Qwen3.6 is the FLAGSHIP scaling of that recipe: 6× total params, 10× activated, 4× layers, but the same 3:1 hybrid pattern and the same DeltaNet kernel. Like Qwen3-Next, no shared expert.

Both are hybrid-attention reasoning MoEs. MiniMax pushes lightning attention to extreme ratios (projected 11:1 lightning:softmax). Qwen3.6 stays moderate at 3:1 — more softmax layers means better short-context quality at higher long-context KV cost. Qwen3.6's linear kernel is Gated DeltaNet (delta-rule with gating); MiniMax uses the Transnormer-style right-product linear attention. Different bets on the linear-attention design space.

V4-Pro keeps full-softmax everywhere but compresses + sparsifies via CSA + HCA + lightning indexer + top-k=1024. Qwen3.6 takes the orthogonal route: replace 75% of attention with O(N) linear attention. Both reach 1M-class context but with different cost profiles — V4 is read-bandwidth-bound on the indexer GEMM, Qwen3.6 is sequential-state-update-bound on the DeltaNet layers.

parts.name: Input Tokens (V≈152K, 200+ langs)
parts.role: Multilingual BPE tokeniser inherited from Qwen3 (~152K vocab).

parts.name: Embedding (V≈152K, d=4096)
parts.role: Tied embedding / LM head.

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=4096, 192 layers)
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm residual carry through 192 layers.

parts.name: × 192 Layers (3 LINEAR : 1 SOFTMAX)
parts.role: Marker: 192 layers = 144 Gated DeltaNet (linear) + 48 Gated Attention (softmax).

parts.name: Block 1 — LINEAR (Gated DeltaNet)
parts.role: Gated DeltaNet block. Linear attention with delta-rule update + gating. NO KV cache — carries a recurrent state instead.

parts.name: Block 2 — LINEAR
parts.role: Linear block 2.

parts.name: Block 3 — LINEAR
parts.role: Linear block 3.

parts.name: Block 4 — SOFTMAX (Gated Attn)
parts.role: Gated Attention block — full softmax over the 256K context with a learned gate on the output. GQA(64/4). Carries the KV cache.

parts.name: Block 5 — LINEAR
parts.role: Linear block 5.

parts.name: Block 6 — LINEAR
parts.role: Linear block 6.

parts.name: Block 8 — SOFTMAX
parts.role: Second softmax block. (Layer 7 linear elided.)

parts.name: Block 192 — SOFTMAX (final)
parts.role: Final softmax block before the LM head.

parts.name: DeltaNet · QKV + α-gate Proj
parts.role: DeltaNet projects to Q, K, V plus a per-token gate α ∈ (0,1) that controls how much of the new K/V replaces the old in the recurrent state (the 'delta rule').
parts.algorithm:
Q, K, V = projections(x)
α      = sigmoid(x @ W_alpha)   # per-token gate

parts.name: Gated DeltaNet Kernel
parts.role: Linear attention with delta-rule update: S_t = (1 - α_t·K_t·K_t^T)·S_{t-1} + α_t·K_t·V_t^T. Per-token cost is O(d_k·d_v), independent of context length.
parts.flops_per_token: ≈ 4·d_k·d_v ≈ low MFLOP — basically free vs softmax
parts.memory_footprint: S: d_k × d_v per layer per sequence ≈ 1 MB / sequence / layer
parts.algorithm:
# per token (decode):
S = (I - α_t * outer(K_t, K_t)) @ S + α_t * outer(K_t, V_t)
out_t = Q_t @ S
# prefill: chunked-parallel over chunks, sequential across
parts.bottleneck: Sequential along time at decode — chunked prefill recovers parallelism.
improvement_ideas: FP8 state with periodic FP16 re-stabilisation.
Multi-head DeltaNet with shared keys for cheaper updates.

parts.name: DeltaNet State (S, fixed-size)
parts.role: Per-sequence per-layer recurrent state. Size INDEPENDENT of context length — what makes 1M context affordable on the 144 linear layers.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 1 MB per sequence per layer, regardless of T

parts.name: DeltaNet · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=4096.

parts.name: Softmax · QKV Proj (GQA 64/4) + Output Gate
parts.role: QKV for the 1-in-4 softmax layers. 64 Q heads share 4 KV heads. PROJECTED 'Gated Attention' adds a learned gate on the output (Qwen3-Next style).
parts.algorithm:
Q, K, V = projections(x)
Q, K = qk_rmsnorm(Q, K)
Q, K = apply_rope(Q, K)
attn = flash_attn(Q, K, V, mask=causal)
gate = sigmoid(x @ W_gate)
out  = gate * (attn @ W_O)

parts.name: Softmax KV Cache (~48 layers only)
parts.role: Standard GQA(4) KV cache for the 48 softmax layers. Linear layers carry no cache.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 1 KB / token / softmax-layer × 48 layers × 256K ctx ≈ 12 GiB / sequence

parts.name: Top-8 Softmax Router (loss-free balance)
parts.role: Per-token softmax over 256 experts, top-8.

parts.name: Routed Experts (256, top-8, NO shared)
parts.role: Per-MoE-layer bank of 256 fine-grained SwiGLU experts. No shared expert (Qwen3 design preserved).
parts.memory_footprint: 256 × 3·d·d_ff × BF16 ≈ 9.3 GiB / layer; 192 layers ≈ 1.78 TiB of routed FFN weights

parts.name: Expert All-to-All
parts.role: NCCL all-to-all dispatch + combine.

parts.name: Dual-Chunk Attention + YaRN (256K → 1M)
parts.role: Inference-time context extension — applied only on the 48 softmax layers.

parts.name: Mode: /think
parts.role: Reasoning mode (preserved from Qwen3).

parts.name: Mode: /no_think
parts.role: Direct-answer mode.

parts.name: LM Head (tied)
parts.role: Tied projection back to ~152K vocab.

parts.name: MTP Head (depth=1, projected newly added)
parts.role: PROJECTED: Qwen3 didn't have MTP at flagship; Qwen3-Coder added one. Qwen3.6 likely brings it to the main flagship for ~1.8× speculative decoding.

parts.name: LM Loss
parts.role: Pre-training next-token cross-entropy + RL post-train rewards.

parts.name: AdamW
parts.role: Single AdamW for all parameters. ZeRO-1 sharding across DP.

parts.name: Post-train: SFT + RLHF + Reasoning-RL + Long-Output RL
parts.role: Multi-stage post-training; projected addition of long-output RL specifically for /think mode at 1M context.

parts.name: Parallelism Plan: TP × EP × PP × DP
parts.role: Linear layers parallelise differently from softmax — sequence-parallel chunked prefill + state-passing across PP boundaries.

parts.name: ~50 T-token Multilingual Corpus (200+ langs)
parts.role: Projected pre-training corpus, expanded language coverage.
